title: Writing - Part 1: Digital Privacy
description: Your class has been discussing the growing concerns about digital privacy and how personal data is used by companies and governments.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the concerns in your notes. You should explain which concern you consider most serious, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Tech companies collect vast amounts of personal data without users fully understanding how it is used.
Government monitoring of citizens, even for security reasons, threatens civil liberties.
Young people share too much personal information on social media without considering the risks.

structure.intro: Introduce the issue of digital privacy and state the two concerns you will discuss.
structure.body1: Discuss the first concern with specific examples of how it affects people.
structure.body2: Discuss the second concern and compare its seriousness to the first.
structure.conclusion: State which concern you regard as most serious and why.

content.modelAnswer:
In the digital age, privacy has become a fundamental concern. As we spend more of our lives online, the risks to our personal information have grown considerably. Two particularly significant concerns are the collection of personal data by corporations and the risks associated with social media use.

Technology companies routinely collect detailed information about users' habits, preferences and locations. This data is often sold to advertisers or used to manipulate consumer behaviour in ways that users are unaware of. Many people accept lengthy terms and conditions without reading them, effectively surrendering their right to privacy. This lack of transparency is deeply troubling.

Social media also poses significant risks, particularly for young people who share personal details publicly without considering the consequences. Private information can be misused by strangers, employers or even criminals. Once something is posted online, it can be nearly impossible to remove.

In my opinion, corporate data collection is the more serious concern. While individuals can choose what they share on social media, they often have little control over the data that companies gather automatically. Greater regulation and clearer consent processes are needed to protect citizens from having their private information exploited without their knowledge.
